nodes.name: Blackwater Crossing
nodes.description: A walled frontier town at the intersection of the Old Northern Trade Road and the Blackwater River, built on both banks and connected by an old stone bridge. Farmland rings the town. Inside: The Laughing Basilisk (party home base), The Dragon's Breath Inn, The Sweaty Mermaid (east-side tavern above the vampire tunnels), The Twist (thieves' guild alleys), Ironfang Crossroads, Hollowstone Square, Oathkeeper's Square, Ebonspire Corner, Shadowveil Crossing, Duskhaven Court, a temple, a market, a cursed well, and old tunnels beneath the streets. Guard Captain Ulf oversees town security. The starting point of the Basilisk Campaign.

nodes.name: The Laughing Basilisk
nodes.description: A tavern near the north gate of Blackwater Crossing and the party's declared home base. Mustering from the Basilisk lets characters roll Hearts with advantage — the place restores them in a way few frontier inns do.

nodes.name: The Sweaty Mermaid
nodes.description: A dockside tavern on the east (bad) side of the Blackwater, tangled in the suspicious-meat rumor. In Session 43 the party tore down the crumbling building beside it, fought red-eyed rats, and found stairs leading down to the old tunnels and the vampire brothel below. A demon desk clerk greets arrivals; townspeople pass through in pseudonyms as clients or chattel.

nodes.name: The Twist
nodes.description: A labyrinth of alleys near the Laughing Basilisk, held by a local canting crew — the town's thieves guild. Easy to get lost in, easier still to be found by the wrong people.

nodes.name: Tunnels Beneath Blackwater Crossing
nodes.description: Old tunnels beneath the city, entered through stairs under a crumbling building next to the Sweaty Mermaid. Just past a beaded curtain arch lies a vampire brothel — receptionist with a giant red-eyed rat, multiple rooms, at least one vampire, and a noble patron who buys blood before each new moon. The fog's Mist Hounds serve the vampires here, and children vanish above to feed them.

nodes.name: The Cursed Farmhouse
nodes.description: A ruined farmstead south of Blackwater Crossing along the Trade Road, scorched by an old fire. A basement still opens beneath the blackened timbers. Something cursed lingers in the ash — locals give it a wide berth.

nodes.name: The Graveyard
nodes.description: A transitional landmark where the Trade Road leaves the Old Forest and descends into farmland — two hours south of the forest edge. Leaning stones and sparse trees mark civilization's northern limit. A gallows further south carries a sign reading "THEEF." Travelers here turn east to reach Kalla Cave.

nodes.name: Kalla Cave
nodes.description: A cave four hours east of the Graveyard, stocked with a large supply of kalla. A stream winds through the hex toward the mouth. The surviving Mud Tooth Porcs — Soak Tusk, Warm Wallow, Steam Snout, Mud Plume and Bubble Trotter — resettled here after Whip convinced them to abandon the Mud Wallow. They lost a sibling, Sulphur Bristle, to the basilisk on the road south, and remain deeply shaken.

nodes.name: The Mud Wallow
nodes.description: A sulfur-smelling hot spring west of the Trade Road, also called the Pork Swither Hot Spring Mud Pool. A stream of steaming water runs east across the road. The therapeutic mud heals 1 Heart per hour of soaking. Former territory of the Mud Tooth Porcs, who were squeezed out by giant spiders, the Feather Clan, and a nearby Pine Troll (HD 6, regenerates, fears fire).

nodes.name: Pjörk Choppe Hille
nodes.description: An organized porc settlement in the black pines roughly 3/4 of a day north of Blackwater Crossing, then an hour west by the widest cart path. Narrower trails take three to six hours; the wide cart path exists because these porcs carry off stolen wagons whole. A recurring regional threat, with raiding forces disciplined enough to cut proper roads.

nodes.name: The Spider Cave
nodes.description: A cave system near the summit of Pjörk Choppe Hille — about an hour's scramble over rocks from the settlement. Daemon spiders with writings on their chitin nest above; below wind tunnels to a skeletal lord's throne room, an Evil Chaos Shrine, a store room, and a torture chamber. The party has used it as a base camp across several expeditions. Slavers led by Painweaver ("Suffering Teaches") reportedly hold caves on the southern side near the cursed spiders.

nodes.name: Crag Cairn
nodes.description: A barrow mound southeast of the Mud Wallow and two hours north of the Kobold Crevasse. On a throne of bones sits the Crag King, a barrow wight chieftain, flanked by eight skeleton guards. The dead zone around the mound is growing. A Thornespire Keep helmet lies among the bones — a trophy from the missing patrol. No trail leads here; the Mud Tooth Porcs guided the party in around the spider nests. A Level 5 encounter the party scouted but did not engage.

nodes.name: The Kobold Crevasse
nodes.description: A minecraft-deep crevasse east of the Trade Road, with a path winding down to a kobold settlement. At the surface stands a mystic ring of trees around a stone table carved with thaumaturgical runes — a binding spell for receiving prophetic dreams as the moon's shadow crosses it. Home to Yip-Snap the hunter and her clan under Chieftain Snarl-Tooth; the kobolds communicate and trash-talk the porcs by drum. A kobold kit was recently taken by spiders from a nearby drum-post, leaving only web.

nodes.name: The Fae Glade
nodes.description: Old-growth forest hidden between Pjörk Choppe Hille and Thornespire Keep, also called The Garden. Silver- and white-barked trees seem to wear faces; curated paths wind between them. Time runs wrong inside — minutes here pass as hours outside. No trail leads in; the party stumbled onto it while crossing between the porc hill and the keep, and the way back cannot be counted on.

nodes.name: Thornespire Keep
nodes.description: A stone fortress atop a tall hill in the Old Forest, commanding views of the river valley and the Trade Road below. Roughly 1.5 days from Blackwater Crossing — a day north to the forest, then four hours west on a well-maintained side road. Contains an Outer Bailey with a Traveler's Inn, the Chapel of Muir, and an Inner Bailey where the Castellan governs. Multicolored lights dance between the towers at night; shared dreams of a brass door beneath the keep grow sharper, and a crack has opened in the foundation directly above where the door is said to lie. Something scrapes in the deep cellars.

nodes.name: Raven's Perch
nodes.description: An abandoned watchtower in the deep Old Forest, reached by a side road that branches west off the Trade Road north of the Thornespire turnoff. The Castellan's scouts vanished here; the party investigated, rescued three of them, and earned a feast at the Inner Bailey and Inner Bailey passes. The manifest for a later "Strange Shipment" (ritual silver daggers for the Vault of First Light) was also recovered from this tower.

nodes.name: The Swamp
nodes.description: Flooded wetlands northwest of Thornespire Keep, at the upstream headwaters of the Blackwater River. Lizardmen have grown more aggressive. A crazy old hermit drinks ice wine and knows things — he has been seen trading for supplies, asking about the cursed well and the murdered guards, and performing rituals at the waterline on full moons. Strange lights pulse deep in the water at night, answering when he paddles toward them. The region was inhabited before the flood; treasure-hunters believe much sank with it.

nodes.name: Mistwood Glen
nodes.description: A mystical sanctuary 2–3 days southwest of Blackwater Crossing through tough forest. The Kalla'din is a pocket realm woven between three house-wide oaks — platforms twenty feet above scorched ground, floating rune-stones, upward-flowing waterfalls, and silver-bark trees growing both upright and inverted. Home to seven immortal Dwur Eldar of the Thornbeard Circle under Gunnar "Brambleheart," now trapped in blissful captivity by stonedcunning fungi. Snezynja, an Aethiiri sorceress bound in starlight chains, keeps ancient memories of the Vault of First Light. The Stone-Nose Clan forest trolls under Grandmother Lichen-Beard dwell at the Standing Stones nearby.

nodes.name: Mountain Watch Inn
nodes.description: Brunhilde Ironfoot's inn and recruiting camp, set in the foothills right on the Trade Road before Serpent's Pass. A moat surrounds the camp — a gelatinous ooze once haunted it, drowning Hawk before the party cleared it out. Brunhilde recruits for the Mountain Watch; lightning blights and wyverns trouble the foothills, and dwur patrols range nearby. Baron Thornwise (gold wheat sheaf on blue) claims jurisdiction through a tax collector.

nodes.name: Serpent's Pass
nodes.description: The narrow mountain pass where the Trade Road threads the Serpent's Teeth Crags, roughly two days south of Blackwater Crossing. Perilous terrain between jagged peaks; the crags themselves span a week of travel in either direction and hide gargoyles, an obsidian quarry, and brigands. Side routes from the pass lead overland to the Vault of First Light.

nodes.name: Bandit Ambush Hill
nodes.description: A rocky Weathertop-like hill on the west edge of the pass, strewn with stones and natural cover — an ideal camp for a small army. The Hangman Hill bandits used it as an ambush perch over the Trade Road, linked back to their water grotto by an underground streambed. The position was finally broken when lightning from the Tower of the Stargazer destroyed the camp.

nodes.name: Tower of the Stargazer
nodes.description: An 80-foot stone tower with no visible windows, crowned by a layered metal dome like a globe and ringed with steel spikes; four 40-foot spikes lean inward from the blasted lunar ground fifty feet out. Lightning strikes constantly, even under clear skies. Two days south of Blackwater Crossing, then half a day west into the foothills. Home of Sir Uravulon Calcidius, an evil wizard corrupted by contact with other worlds and trapped in a salt circle some 59 years ago by his servant Argyle Timmons, who stole his spellbook and fled. His senile fumbling is a mask — once freed he is imperious and murderous.

nodes.name: Vault of First Light
nodes.description: An ancient dwarven dungeon reached by two days south on the Trade Road and a further day overland through hills and up a pass between the Serpent's Teeth. Rooms, traps, and the Fallen — undead dwur miners — guard ties to older cosmic forces. The party has already explored it and brought back an eternal candle that speaks in ancient tongues; scholars and treasure-hunters have since flocked to Blackwater Crossing. The ritual silver daggers recovered from Raven's Perch are the keys to deeper chambers.

nodes.name: The North Road
nodes.description: The Trade Road passes beyond the known map here, pressed in on both sides by dense Old Forest. The way is well-maintained as far as explored territory goes, but no one the party has spoken with has reliable report of what lies further north into the Belerion wilds.

nodes.name: The South Pass
nodes.description: Where the Trade Road emerges from the Serpent's Teeth pass at the southern edge of the known map, descending out of the mountains toward unknown lands beyond.

nodes.name: Northern Warding Stone
nodes.description: A carved rune-stone standing west of the Trade Road near the Mud Wallow — one of the Vampire Warding Stones. Only two have been found so far; the pattern is likely an arc or ring rather than a pair. The warding is believed tied to the vampire brothel beneath Blackwater Crossing and possibly to a larger ley-line network, and its runes resemble those on the Kobold Crevasse stone table and the seal of the Silent Eater.

nodes.name: Southern Warding Stone
nodes.description: A carved rune-stone where the Blackwater River enters the Old Forest from the northwest — the second known Vampire Warding Stone. Its runes match older carvings tied to the Silent Eater's seal and the stone table at the Kobold Crevasse. The full set is unknown; more likely stand undiscovered elsewhere in the forest.

nodes.name: The Basilisk Spiderwood
nodes.description: A deep wood southwest of the Mud Wallow where the basilisk's territory overlaps with giant spider nests. The Mud Tooth Porcs call the clearing at its heart "the quiet ground" and lost kin there. Petrified figures stand in the webs — including the naturalist Saerin Voth, caught studying the stone garden — proof the basilisk was here long before the spiders. The "basilisk" and the brood-mother spider are one and the same: an ancient eight-legged priestess-guardian feeding a pre-Dwur seal beneath the forest, and each victim thickens the binding.

nodes.name: Old Forest Edge
nodes.description: The waypoint where the Trade Road crosses from tilled farmland into dense Old Forest, about a day's march (18 miles) north of Blackwater Crossing. The treeline curves away east and west — a prominent band of tall black pines marking the edge of civilized country.

nodes.name: Bandit Camp
nodes.description: A large bandit operation an hour north of the Tower of the Stargazer, just inside the forest's southern edge — 120 people in all, 48 bandits and 72 slaves. Krin "The Cunning" led a 20-strong strike force; Rath "Iron-Will" ran the slave-pit and quarry, waving a dubious writ for "contract labor"; Corth "Whip-Crack" never spoke. The obsidian quarry is a shale crater around a single dark spire — possibly hollow, possibly deeper than it looks — and digging at its base was forbidden on pain of death. Carved serpent marks on the trees hinted at plans beyond lumber. Lightning from the Tower eventually reduced the camp to ash; captives were freed, and the party earned 4 XP in Session 42.

nodes.name: Maret's Charcoal Mound
nodes.description: At the tree-line where the Trade Road enters the wood, a slow-burning charcoal mound exhales smoke that smells of rendered fat rather than timber — tended by a heavy-set old woman who gives only the name Maret and deflects every question about the ring of antler-tip stakes she has pressed into the black loam around the mound's base.

nodes.name: Merrow's Barley Fields
nodes.description: Half a mile east of Blackwater's outer farms, a scarecrow in the center of Merrow's largest plot stands dressed in a genuine town guard's tabard — the star-and-river badge still legible, the cloth unwashed and fresh. A girl is watching from the field edge with a mattock across her shoulders, and she won't meet your eyes.

nodes.name: Crookstaff Farm
nodes.description: Visible from the south-east track, a barn door is daubed with a red handprint — the tribute mark Krin's bandits leave when a farmer has paid once and agreed to keep paying. The owner, a wide-shouldered woman named Dotta, stands in the doorway with a grain flail across her knees; she paid the first time, swore never again, and has been watching the southern road every morning since.

nodes.name: Tula's Outlook
nodes.description: A flat-topped spur of rock right on the north-west approach, where a half-deaf woman named Tula Oss has built a low stone windbreak and spends her days watching Serpent's Pass through a dented brass spyglass — her greasy ledger logs every traveller by description and time, and she will not say who she reports to.

nodes.name: Nob's Rise
nodes.description: The first hill that lifts you clear of Merrow's barley country, crowned by a drover's cairn built over a bleached horse skull — each traveler adds a stone for luck. A woman sits at its base fletching arrows without looking up; her boots are soaked through, and the nearest ford is two hours back the way you came.

nodes.name: Bram's Spiral Field
nodes.description: Cresting the gentle ridge from the forest edge, the first thing visible is a broad field plowed in tight concentric spirals instead of rows — Bram Kessel has worked it this way for three weeks, since his wife scratched the same pattern into the hearthstone the morning she vanished. He's on his ninth pass and won't answer questions until the ox stops.

nodes.name: The Drowned Stone
nodes.description: Where the Blackwater bends sharply south, a carved standing stone rises from mid-river — water churning white around its base, the lower half swallowed by weed and silt. Only the top two runes break the surface, and they match the warding stones to the north exactly, placing a third marker in the arc long before the river shifted to claim it.

nodes.name: Orro's Survey Line
nodes.description: A dead-straight line of blazed beeches descends the hillside toward the north-west — each trunk scored with the same spiral cut at chest height, the work of weeks. At the line's lower end sits Orro Bas, a lean surveyor counting tree-rings on a fresh-cut stump, who will not say why the line must reach the valley floor before midsummer.

1107.number: 2d6 → 7 farmers armed with billhooks, one with a hunting bow
1107.activity: Patrolling the furrow-lines at the field's eastern edge, watching the treeline toward the Old Forest for kobold raiders out of the Crevasse
1107.reaction: Unfriendly — they've lost grain stores and one farmhand to kobold night-raids this fortnight and trust no armed strangers crossing their land at dusk
1107.description: Seven gaunt farmers fan out across the cabbage rows in a loose skirmish line, billhooks held at the low carry, torches unlit but tucked in belts — and every pair of eyes locks onto the party the moment they crest the rise.

0505.rumor: Drubwick, the one-eyed fen-poler who works the deep crossing, sells knotted reed charms to anyone fool enough to wade west of the third marker. Won't say what they're charming against, but he mutters that the mud bubbles even in a hard frost out there — warm, like something breathing underneath. The reeds around the old sunken stonework grow twice the height of anything else in the fen.
0505.source: Drubwick, a one-eyed fen-poler who guides bog crossings for a copper a head

0905.rumor: Marta Klöv — she tends the Northern Warding Stone, same as her mother before her — came back from her third-moon visit white as new tallow and wouldn't meet anyone's eyes. Sold off her goats by morning. Folk in Pjörk Choppe Hille say the stone's cold glow hasn't been seen since the last hard frost, and that the webs on the north trail are thicker than any season prior.
0905.source: A limping tallow-chandler nursing sour ale near the fire in Pjörk Choppe Hille
